posts.title: Hello World
posts.text: Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find the answer in troubleshooting or you can ask me on GitHub. Quick StartCreate a new post1$ hexo new "My New Post" More info: Writing Run server1$ hexo server More info: Server Generate static files1$ hexo generate More info: Generating Deploy to remote sites1$ hexo deploy More info: Deployment

posts.title: Idea and thought for Cubic Newton
posts.text: Cubic NewtonAfter have a conversation with Bo.J, I’ve change my direction to Cubic Newton which is a second-order algorithm. I’ve read Cubic Regularization of Newton written by Nestrov, and there are kinds of preponderence of CR: CR has a faster global convergence rate than Gradient Descent and local quadratic convergence. CR can jump out of Saddle point. CR have a great result in some weak convex problem such as Star Convex CR can keep necessary second order optimality condition. Difficulty of this algorithm is its subproblem, as this subproblem is a 1 dimenstional eqution with some tricky nonlinear function. The Tradtional method is Krylov method with Lanczos subspace (but acutally I don’t really know these methods) Then A new method is Adaptive Cubic Regularization (ACR), and I’m reading relatives paper.
